item.title: How tone-deaf can you be? Why would you come here after that?
item.description: "This morning, Sugarbush snow reporter Lucy Welch took the opportunity to make some good trouble by sending out a message of resistance against Vance and the administration he represents. The message went out via email to all Sugarbush daily report subscribers and appeared on the website for a brief time before it was removed. Here is the text of her message: Mar 1st, 2025, 6:49 AM: Today of all days, I would like to reflect on what Sugarbush means to me. This mountain has brought me endless days of joy, adventure, challenges, new experiences, beauty, community, and peace. I’ve found that nothing cures a racing mind quite like skiing through the trees and stopping to take a deep breath of that fresh forest air. The world around us might be a scary place, but these little moments of tranquility, moments I’ve been fortunate enough to enjoy as a direct result of my employment here, give me, and I’d guess you, too, a sense of strength and stability. This fresh forest air, is, more specifically fresh National Forest air. Sugarbush operates on 1745 acres of the Green Mountain National Forest. Right now, National Forest lands and National Parks are under direct attack by the current Administration, who is swiftly terminating the positions of dedicated employees who devote their lives to protecting the land we love, and to protecting us while we are enjoying that land. This Administration also neglects to address the danger, or even the existence of, climate change, the biggest threat to the future of our industry, and the skiing we all so much enjoy here. Burlington, VT is one of the fastest-warming cities in the country, and Vermont is the 9th fastest-warming state. The National Oceanic and Atmospheric Association (NOAA), a resource I use every day for snow reporting, is crucial in monitoring extreme weather events and informing public safety measures, and is also experiencing widespread layoffs and defunding at the hands of the Administration. Sugarbush would not be Sugarbush without our wonderful community. Employees and patrons alike, we are made up of some of the most kind hearted, hardworking people I have ever met. Our community is rich with folks of all different orientations, ethnicities, and walks of life, who all contribute to make this place what it is. They all love Sugarbush because it is a place where they can come to move their bodies, to connect with the land, to challenge themselves, to build character, to nourish their souls with the gift of skiing. Many of these people are part of the LGBTQI+ community. Many (well, that’s a stretch, we all know this is an incredibly white-washed industry) are people of color. Half are women. Many are veterans or adaptive skiers who, through Vermont Adaptive, are able to access snow sports in part thanks to federal grants through the Department of Veterans Affairs, which is also facing devastating cuts. Many of our beloved employees moved across the world through an exchange program on the J1 visa to help this resort run, and they are not US citizens. ALL of these groups are being targeted, undervalued, and disrespected by the current Administration. The beauty of National Forest land, is that anyone and everyone is welcome to enjoy it. Anyone and everyone can buy a lift ticket. I also imagine it is incredibly difficult, and likely impossible, to say “No” to the Secret Service. I hope that, instead of faulting Sugarbush management or employees for “allowing this to happen”, you can direct your anger to the source — the Administration that, in my oh-so-humble opinion, is threatening our democracy, our livelihoods, our land. I want to reiterate how much I admire and respect my fellow employees and managers — they work so hard to make this place operate, to keep you coming back and enjoying it and making lifelong memories. Many of them may feel the same way that I do, but their hands are tied, and for good reason. They have families to support, they have benefits and health insurance to receive, they face far greater and more binding pressure from Corporate. I am in a privileged position here, in that I work only seasonally, I do not rely on this job for health insurance or benefits, and hey, waking up at 4:30 AM isn’t exactly sustainable. Therefore, I am using my relative “platform” as snow reporter, to be disruptive — I don’t have a whole lot to lose. We are living in a really scary and really serious time. What we do or don’t do, matters. This whole shpiel probably won’t change a whole lot, and I can only assume that I will be fired, but at least this will do even just a smidge more than just shutting up and being a sheep. I am really scared for our future. Acting like nothing is happening here feels way scarier than losing my job. I want to have kids one day, and I want to teach them to ski. The policies and ideals of the current Administration, however, are not conducive to either of these things, because, at least how things look now, I’d never be able to afford a good life for a child anyway, and snow will be a thing of Vermont history. So please, for the sake of our future shredders: Be Better Here. It has truly been a pleasure writing your morning snow reports — I hope this one sticks with you. With love, peace, and hope, Lucy Welch But hey, while we’re here…1-3” of fresh snow to kick off this interesting weekend. Chance of scattered mixed precip showers today, with warmer temps reaching 36 at the base and 28 at the summit. Right now, the snowpack is a mix of machine groomed and frozen granular, with more winter-like conditions near the summits, but the new snow may help nudge conditions into the powder/packed powder category in certain aspects and elevations. Enjoy 60 groomers and 100% open terrain today! For Saturday, we’ll be rocking 111 trails, 484 skiable acres, and 60 groomed runs. Temps are expected to be in the mid-20s and mid-30s under cloudy skies with winds out of the WNW ranging from 15-40 MPH. With all the new snow we saw this month, it is more important than ever to be diligent when skiing and riding in the woods — tree wells pose a greater risk with all this fresh pow. Thank you, Lucy — that was wonderful." A little long; too important. She still gave the weather report ˵ ͡° ͜ʖ ͡°˵ He had to leave to an "undisclosed location," btw. The real people care. New England cares about the right things for the most part. Only place I feel safe rn. And a link I guess: https://ftw.usatoday.com/story/sports/outdoors/2025/03/01/jd-vance-family-protestors-vermont-ski-vacation/80983762007/

item.title: Ostriches, scroll past; everyone else, listen: some new shit from the AmeriKKKastan is impacting people globally, start reaching out to some of these groups to see who you can help
item.description:
Source for those of you who recognize that (a) Imgur ≠ self-care, (b) anyone that actually cares about their mental health would seek out a professional, not go to social media, and (c) it's important to keep up-to-date on current events so you're not caught off-guard when it eventually reaches you: https://apnews.com/article/usaid-cuts-hunger-sickness-288b1d3f80d85ad749a6d758a778a5b2 Also, as user @FishieStardust pointed out, "if you want to read a post, but can't handle it in the moment, create a folder for things you want to look at later. Hold down the heart favorite icon, and add it to that folder". ((thank you for the great suggestion btw!)) For those mobile users who wanna keep your head in the sand, I'll give you this nifty little life hack. - Press your fingertip on the right side of your phone's screen - Keeping the fingertip in direct contact with the screen, move it to the left - Congrats, you now know how to scroll past something you don't want to read.
Remember, if you're in the US, continue to protest and keep yourselves armed! Even if it's just pepper spray and/or a pocketknife, you have the right to peacefully assemble to address your grievances to the government - and the right to protect the ability to continue exercising such a right, too.
If you're not in the US, you have an impact, too! Call your own representatives, PMs, or other public servants in the government; push to have them establish sanctions, tariffs, and more on the US! Our nation is being run by fascists, you canNOT let fascists have their way.
Many of you on this site have criticized Americans for "not doing enough". Well, time to put your money where your mouth is...or, rather, time to give it to these groups in need. Check in with some of these aid groups and see what you can do to support those impacted by the US's shitty government and its enteric actions.
Damn, Ukraine can't catch a break, can they?
Cat tax. Eggothy is quite content, now that he's had his first walk outside since the start of our harsh winters. I just hope I can keep giving him his nice walks and wet food. Things don't look the best for poor people like myself and my girlfriend in this country, but we're gonna do our best. For the Egg.
Integrity is important. Which is why you, the average imgur user, should know that the mods of this site have very little of it. They've now begun deliberately removing tags from posts, not just adding ones on. Posted at 3pm PT, we're gonna keep this screenshot here to see how long it takes them to manipulate information. . . . . . FP edit: well howdy, everyone! Thank you for taking the time to read this far. Please do not give into despair, we *will* make it through this. As long as we don't keep our heads in the sand, we can see what's coming and fight back.
item.dc:creator: goblinwithavendetta

oh no

If you want to learn more and could possibly help me follow the link tree below to the GoFundMe and other places you can find my content https://linktr.ee/GridSquares Folks at this juncture everybody has to do their part. You either stand up against authoritarian or get walked over by it

item.dc:creator: hoopsnek

item.title: It's caturday so here's a dump of my crafty cats
item.description:
Can't do anything without at least one supurrvisor Note- they have never poked themselves on a needle and when coming back up with the point of the needle I do shield it with my hand so they don't pounce on it and poke their beans. No cats have been harmed in the making of these crafts!
Blue is definitely his color. This one's Kona
And he is Tito
No ..it's ok it's not like I needed that fabric...
Often he likes to sit in my lap or on my hands when I'm embroidering. Hands is inconvenient.
They loved this starry fabric so much, and we're being such menaces, that I made them a little cape as revenge with the leftover.
Their nosiness extends to reading
And putting together furniture.
And now we have a new addition, a stray that just decided one day she would not leave untill we let her in and of course she too immediately got to craft supurrvision, with no training needed. We called her a little frog because her meow was croaky and it stuck so her name is Frog aka Fruggie Buggie, or Goblin Menace.
She's a pro
And she is tiny compared to the boys. Vet said she's probably less than 2.
I can't do anything with fabric anymore, gotta fend off all three menaces at once. It's a great problem to have!
item.dc:creator: TheStitchingSquid
